parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces significant changes to the core client, retry, and error-dispatch modules, but glm-client.mjs — the primary API communication layer with 155 lines of changes — has no dedicated test file. Every other core lib module (args, fs, render, state, retry, bigmodel-errors, preset-config) received a corresponding test, making this an asymmetric gap in a module that handles auth tokens, HTTP calls, and error propagation. Combined with the complete deletion of process.mjs and multiple rounds of post-deletion hotfixes (Bundles C–F), the risk of a latent import or integration regression is non-trivial. Recommend adding glm-client unit tests covering error dispatch, retry coordination, and abort paths before shipping.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs has 155 lines of changes but no dedicated test file
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs is the primary HTTP/API client. The diff stat shows 155 lines changed (additions + removals), covering integration with the new retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs modules. Every other changed lib module has a matching test file (args.test.mjs, fs.test.mjs, render.test.mjs, state.test.mjs, retry.test.mjs, bigmodel-errors.test.mjs, preset-config.test.mjs). glm-client.test.mjs does not appear anywhere in the 84-file diff. The client is the convergence point for auth, error dispatch, retry coordination, and job lifecycle — all high-cost failure surfaces. The absence of targeted tests means regressions in API error handling, retry backoff integration, or token propagation would only surface at runtime against a live endpoint.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering: (1) error classification dispatch via bigmodel-errors, (2) retry backoff integration via retry.mjs, (3) auth token propagation, (4) abort/cancellation during in-flight requests, (5) partial response / stream error handling. Mirror the coverage depth of retry.test.mjs (352 lines).

findings.title: process.mjs fully deleted; lingering dynamic imports may cause runtime MODULE_NOT_FOUND
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was completely removed as part of 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge.' The commit message explicitly mentions ReferenceError, confirming the deletion caused runtime failures. Although Bundles E+, D3+, and C followed with fixes, the cumulative diff still shows glm-companion.mjs with 139 lines changed and render.mjs with 79 lines changed — both likely former consumers. If any conditional or lazy import path still references 'process.mjs', the failure will only manifest when that specific code path is exercised (e.g., a rarely-used subcommand or error-recovery branch). No grep or import-check for stale references is visible in the CI scripts.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r "process\.mjs" scripts/ commands/` to confirm no stale references remain. Add a CI guard in check-imports.mjs that fails if any file imports from a module not present in the source tree. Consider adding an integration test that exercises every subcommand to catch latent MODULE_NOT_FOUND errors.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs and job-control.mjs changed without dedicated unit tests
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) and scripts/lib/job-control.mjs (20 lines changed) manage job lifecycle state — start, poll, complete, fail. The test suite includes job-render.test.mjs (144 lines) which likely exercises render paths, but neither module has its own dedicated test file. These modules handle persistent job state; a bug in state transitions (e.g., marking a failed job as complete, or losing track of a running job on process restart) would cause silent corruption of user-visible job tracking with no recovery path.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering: (1) job state machine transitions (pending→running→complete/failed), (2) concurrent job tracking, (3) state persistence and reload, (4) edge cases: empty state file, corrupted state file, orphaned jobs. Verify job-control.mjs is covered via the same or a dedicated test.

next_steps: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs with coverage parity to retry.test.mjs before merging.
Grep entire codebase for any remaining import of 'process.mjs' and add a CI guard in check-imports.mjs to catch stale references.
Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering job lifecycle state transitions and persistence edge cases.
Run the full test suite (`scripts/ci/check-all.sh`) locally and confirm all existing tests pass against the current tree before finalizing the sweep.
